info.title: Onboarding merchants
info.description:
Use the Onboarding API to perform complete merchant management.


> #### Note
> Merchant onboarding can be performed using the API or web portal. To learn about the registration flow on the portal, visit  [Platform](https://lighthouse.dock.tech/docs/your-backoffice/ZG9jOjIxNTU3OTg4-onboarding-merchants) documentation.

<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Atention
>
> Register the merchant and then add the necessary documents for the KYC process.

The merchant onboarding flow begins with the sending of mandatory registration data. Once the merchant is created on our database, it will be necessary to send the mandatory documents for KYC analysis. To learn about the flow and mandatory documents, access the [Platform](https://lighthouse.dock.tech/docs/your-backoffice/ZG9jOjIxNTU3OTg4-onboarding-merchants) documentation.

To monitor the KYC status update, simply consult the merchant and consider the *riskAnalysisStatus* and *riskAnalysisStatusJustification* fields.

post.description: Endpoint used to include a merchant into the system. Before checking all the technical details of the Onboarding endpoint, we recommend that you read all the guidelines on this [page](https://developers.dock.tech/v1-acquiring/reference/onboarding-merchants-api).

Bad Request.

| ERROR CODE        | ERROR MESSAGE        |
|-------------------|----------------------|
| MERCHANT_ALREADY_EXISTS |         This merchant already exists. |
| MSG_MERCHANT_DOCUMENTID_NULL | Merchant documentId can not be null. |
| MSG_MERCHANT_DOCUMENTID_SIZE | Merchant documentId maximum size is 30. |
| MSG_MERCHANT_DONT_HAVE_PARTNER_CONTACT | At least one contact must be designated as a partner or owner. |

errors.msg: Merchant documentId can not be null.

put.description:
Endpoint used to update merchants.

To update the commercial establishment, send a `PUT` request to the `v1/onboarding/{slug}` endpoint, where `{slug}` is unique identifier of the merchant you want to update.

<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Atention
>
> Not all merchant data can be changed due to Authorizer, NÚCLEA, CERC and BACEN rules. If you are unable to change the data on the endpoint, contact Dock.

requestBody.description: Send the data you want to change in the merchant registration.

delete.description:
Endpoint used to remove or deactivate a commercial establishment.

To remove or deactivate a commercial establishment, send a `DELETE` request to the `v1/onboarding/{slug}` endpoint, where the `{slug}` is the unique identifier of the merchant that you want to delete.


<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Atention
>
> The merchant will be removed if they do not have terminals and transactions associated to them. Otherwise they will be deactivated.

200.description: After the call is successfully completed, we will return a message with HTTP 200 code and empty body.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Unique merchant identifier.

parameters.name: slugMerchant
parameters.description: Unique merchant identifier.

post.description:
Endpoint used to add documents to merchants.

To add document, send a `POST` request to the `/v1/merchants/{slugMerchant}/documents/` endpoint, where `{slugMerchant}` is the serial UUID number genereated by the system for the merchant.

parameters.name: X-Customer
parameters.description: Unique customer identifier.

requestBody.description:
The slugs of mandatory documents will be informed according to the needs and context of each client.


<details>
<summary>Document types</summary>

Below, you will find all document types by person type and category. Check the document requirements by accessing our Onboarding documentation at this [link](https://developers.dock.tech/docs/onboarding-de-estabelecimentos). Ensure that the document is submitted under the correct category to enable proper validation.

delete.description:
Endpoint used to remove documents from merchants.

To remove documents from merchants, you must send a `DELETE` request to the `/v1/merchant/{slugMerchant}/documents/{slug}` endpoint , where `{slugMerchant}` is the serial UUID generated by the system for the commercial establishment, and `{slug}` is the system-generated serial UUID for the document to be removed.

<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Atention
>
> If the merchant has KYC Approved status, documents cannot be deleted.

204.description: After the call is successfully completed, you will receive a message with the HTTP 204 code and empty body.

get.description: In this endpoint it is possible to search for all terms that a merchant has. The term is created at the time of onboarding, so consider that each registration will have only one registration, however in certain scenarios the merchant may have more than one term registered.

parameters.name: slugMerchant
parameters.description: Unique merchant identifier.

parameters.name: slugTerm
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the Term.

200.description:
The terms will be presented in PDF format, containing a header that details the parties involved, along with the merchant's registration and commercial data, followed by the agreement's clauses.

To find an example file, please visit our Help Center.

get.description:
This endpoint is responsible for returning the file in PDF format. Use this file to be displayed in your frontend solution provided to your merchants.

The terms will be presented in PDF format, containing a header that details the parties involved, along with the merchant's registration and commercial data, followed by the agreement's clauses.

parameters.name: slugTerm
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the Term.

put.description:
Use this endpoint to sign the merchant term. From the moment a term is signed, the merchant will be able to carry out transactions and access the Dock Acquiring Portal.

<!-- theme: warning -->
> #### Atention
>
> As responsible for the merchant, consider carrying out all necessary validations to ensure that whoever is signing the term is in fact legally responsible for the merchant before sending signature confirmation.

All these validations are necessary to ensure that the person legally responsible for the merchant is aware of all registration and commercial conditions, in addition to agreeing with the clauses described in the term and the contracts that the term references.

userAcceptance.description: Identification of the user who signed the term on your platform. Use CPF or registered email for the *merchant_contact* responsible for the merchant.

requestBody.description: For control and traceability, when signing the term it will be necessary to inform which user is signed.

parameters.name: slugMerchant
parameters.description: Unique merchant identifier.

get.description:
Endpoint used to search for one of your merchants registered in our ecosystem.

You must make an HTTP/HTTPS request to the `/v1/merchants/{slugMerchant}` endpoint, where slug is the serial UUID generated by the system for the merchant.
Endpoint used to return one or more merchants according to the parameters entered. If no parameters are entered, all merchants will be listed.

parameters.name: address.city__like
parameters.description: Search for merchants that are registered in cities that contain the entered text.

parameters.name: address.city
parameters.description: Search for merchants that are registered in cities with the same name as the text entered.

parameters.name: address.state
parameters.description: Search for merchants that are registered in States with the same name as the text entered.

parameters.name: riskAnalysisStatus
parameters.description: Search for merchants according to their KYC status.

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: Search for active or inactive merchants.

parameters.name: documentId
parameters.description: Search for merchant by national registration (CPF or CNPJ).

parameters.name: dtInsert__goe
parameters.description: Search for merchants that were registered on dates greater than the one entered.

parameters.name: dtInsert__loe
parameters.description: Search for merchants that were registered on dates shorter than the one entered.

parameters.name: name__like
parameters.description: Search for merchants that have the name that contains the entered text.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Search for merchants by exact name.

get.description: This endpoint is used to retrieve the bank domicile data associated with a merchant. The bank domicile is the destination bank account (defined by branch and account number) where the merchant receives the funds from their authorized transactions. Using this endpoint is essential to verify the account information that will be used for payment settlements.

put.description:
Use this endpoint to update the settlement bank account information for your merchants. Modifications can range from changing the ownership details registered during onboarding, to simply updating account and branch data, or completely switching the banking institution used for receiving settlements.

To ensure a successful operation, it is crucial that the data provided is correct and matches the ownership (CPF/CNPJ) of the merchant or its parent company. Additionally, you must certify that the destination banking institution is accredited by Núclea, thus ensuring its capability to process receivable settlements for all payment schemes the merchant uses.

The `slug_merchant_bank_account` is the identifier for the bank account you wish to modify. To find it, make a GET request to [the endpoint](https://developers.dock.tech/v1-acquiring/reference/get-v1-merchants-banking-bank_accounts) that lists the merchant's bank accounts and locate the corresponding id in the response.

documentId.description: The account holder's document ID (CNPJ or CPF). Send this field only when you intend to change the account's ownership.

corporateName.description: The account holder's Corporate Name or Full Name. Send this field only when you intend to change the account's ownership.

legalPerson.description: The type of person. Send this field only when you intend to change the account's ownership.

bankBranchNumber.description: The new bank branch number.  If you are not updating this field, do not send it in the request, as null or empty values are not permitted.

bankBranchCheckDigit.description: The new branch number check digit. To remove the existing value, send null.

accountNumber.description: The new account number.  If you are not updating this field, do not send it in the request, as null or empty values are not permitted.

accountNumberCheckDigit.description: The new account number check digit.  If you are not updating this field, do not send it in the request, as null or empty values are not permitted.

accountType.description: The new account type.  If you are not updating this field, do not send it in the request, as null or empty values are not permitted.

compeCode.description: The new bank identifier code (COMPE). Send this field to change the settlement bank. If you are not updating this field, do not send it in the request, as null or empty values are not permitted.

parameters.name: slugMerchant
parameters.description: Unique merchant identifier.

200.description:
OK.
This endpoint does not return a response body for success.

post.description: This endpoint grants full autonomy to manage your merchant status, allowing you to immediately deactivate accounts that are no longer part of your active operation. Once executed, the merchant is blocked from processing new transactions; however, this deactivation can be treated as temporary, giving you the discretion to reactivate the merchant at any time according to your business needs.

requestBody.description: The request body must contain solely the field indicating the reason for the merchant's deactivation.

value.name: Dock
value.corporateName: Dock Soluções em Meios de Pagamento

category.name: Comércio varejista de artigos de óptica

value.riskAnalysisStatusJustification: Após avaliação dos dados enviados verificamos que o perfil deste cadastro não atende à nossa política de credenciamento, portanto o pedido de credenciamento foi rejeitado. A rejeição não pode ser recorrida. Por favor, informe ao estabelecimento comercial que o mesmo não foi aceito por políticas internas da adquirente.

post.description:
This endpoint is designed to reactivate a currently inactive merchant, restoring their permission to operate on the platform. By executing this action, you reverse the previous deactivation status and immediately enable the merchant to process new transactions.

To execute this action, no data is required in the request body. Simply provide the merchant's identification in the URL to revert the status to active.

Content-type.description: Indicates the format of the request body.

documentId.description: National registration (CPF). Validate whether the information provided is correct, as if it is a partner contact, this document will be analyzed according to the PLD rules.

email.description: Contact e-mail. It is important to have an email with full access, as this will be the channel for receiving communications, such as welcome messages and password resets. Do not use emails with the gov.br domain.

phoneType.description: The type of number. Options: C - Cellphone, P - Phone

mothersName.description: Full name of the contact's mother.

isPartnerContact.description: Sets the contact as the merchant owner. It is necessary to have at least one person marked as partner contact.

isPep.description: Defines the contact as a politically exposed person.

slugRole.description: Identification of the access profile the user will have when accessing the Dock Acquiring portal. This parameter is mandatory if the merchant user will have access to the Dock Acquiring portal.

complement.description: Description of the address complement if any.

neighborhood.description: Description of the name of the neighborhood.

name.description: Full name of the contact person or legal representative of the merchant.

documentId.description: National registration (CPF) of the person responsible for the merchant. Make sure to provide the correct information, as this document will be used in PLD analysis.

email.description: Contact email. Make sure the responsible person has full access to the inbox, as this will be the channel for notifications and flows such as password reset and welcome messages. Do not use emails with the gov.br domain.

phoneType.description: The type of number. Options: C - Cellphone, P - Phone

mothersName.description: Full name of the contact's mother.

isPartnerContact.description: Indicates whether the contact is the legal representative of the merchant. It is mandatory to provide at least one legal representative.

isPep.description: Defines the contact as a politically exposed person.

slugRole.description: Identification of the access profile the user will have when accessing the Dock Acquiring portal. This parameter is mandatory if the merchant user will have access to the Dock Acquiring portal.

category.name: Comércio varejista de artigos de óptica

slug.description: The unique identifier of the commercial establishment.

active.description: Informs whether the merchant is active.

merchantId.description: Merchant identifier for synchronization with authorizers (MID).

dtUpdate.description: Date of the last update of the merchant registration.

name.description: The trade name of the merchant.

corporateName.description: The company name of the merchant.
examples: Dock Soluções em Meios de Pagamento

email.description: The e-mail to contact the merchant.

timezone.description: The default timezone in which the merchant operates.

areaCode.description: Area code of the merchant's contact number.

phoneType.description: The type of number. Options: C - Cellphone, P - Phone.

legalPerson.description: The type of legal entity of the merchant.

inclusion.description: Registration of the user responsible for merchant registration. If registering via the Portal, the login email will be returned.

openingDays.description: The days when the merchant is open. The field format is a 7-character String, with each character '0' or '1' referencing a day of the week. For example, “0111110" merchant is open from Monday to Friday, except Saturday and Sunday.

openingHour.description: The opening hours of the merc

closingHour.description: The closing hour of the merchant.

hasTef.description: Indicates whether the merchant has TEF enabled.

hasPix.description: Indicates whether the merchant has PIX enabled.

hasTop.description: Indicates whether the merchant has Tap On Phone enabled.

revenue.description: Amount in reais of the merchant's annual revenue.

termSigned.description: Indicates whether the merchant is required to sign the acceptance agreement or not.

has3ds.description: Indicates whether the merchant is registered to operate with 3DS for the MasterCard brand.

Example 1.name: Dock
Example 1.corporateName: Dock Soluções em Meios de Pagamento

riskAnalysisStatusJustification.description: Informative text explaining the reason for the KYC review status.
riskAnalysisStatusJustification.example: Após avaliação dos dados enviados verificamos que o perfil deste cadastro não atende à nossa política de credenciamento, portanto o pedido de credenciamento foi rejeitado. A rejeição não pode ser recorrida. Por favor, informe ao estabelecimento comercial que o mesmo não foi aceito por políticas internas da adquirente.

email.description: The e-mail to contact the merchant.

areaCode.description: Area code of the merchant's contact number.

phoneType.description: The type of number. Options: C - Cellphone, P - Phone.

address.description: Full address where the merchant is located.

contacts.description: List of people responsible for the merchant. At least one must be the partner or owner.

timezone.description: The default timezone in which the merchant operates.

isMainOffice.description: Identifies whether the merchant is a branch.

legalPerson.description: The type of legal entity of the merchant.

openingDays.description: The days when the merchant is open. The field format is a 7-character String, with each character '0' or '1' referencing a day of the week. For example, “0111110" merchant is open from Monday to Friday, except Saturday and Sunday.

hasPix.description: Indicates whether the merchant has PIX enabled.

hasTop.description: Indicates whether the merchant has Tap On Phone enabled.

has3ds.description: Identifies whether the merchant will have 3DS. There may be additional costs for the MasterCard brand. Please contact us if you have any questions.

revenue.description: Amount in reais of the merchant's annual revenue.

merchant-Pix-Account.description: Object listed for merchants with PIX functionality enabled.
merchant-Pix-Account.title: merchant-pix-account

slug.description: Unique object identifier.

active.description: Identification of whether the object is active for the merchant.

dtInsert.description: Date of insertion of PIX data for the merchant.

dtUpdate.description: Date the object was last updated.

idRegistration.description: Unique identifier of the intermediary account record in the database.

bankBranchDigit.description: Bank branch digit number, if any.

bankAccountDigit.description: Bank account digit number, if any.

bankAccountType.description: Identification of the type of bank account.

bankAccountStatus.description: Description of the status of the bank account generated to intermediate the merchant's PIX operation. Sales via PIX are only successful if the account status is ACTIVE.

onboardingPixStatus.description: Description of the merchant's onboarding status at the bank that mediates PIX operations. The intermediary account will only be created when the onboarding status is ACTIVE.

message.description: In cases of failure or business rule, this field will return the last message of the action needed to progress in the onboarding process.

bankName.description: Name of the bank where the intermediary account was generated.

mainOffice-request.description: If the registered merchant is a branch, this object must be provided with data from the merchant mainoffice.
mainOffice-request.title: Main Office Request

slug.description: Unique identifier of the parent merchant.

merchant-configuration-response.description: Merchant configuration response model. Object will be displayed if the merchant has the CNP configuration.
merchant-configuration-response.title: merchant-configuration-response

slug.description: Unique identifier of the object.

active.description: Identification of whether the object is active.

dtInsert.description: Date the configuration was entered for the merchant.

dtUpdate.description: Date the object was last updated.

lockCpAnticipationOrder.description: Informs whether the merchant has anticipation blocking for CP transactions.

lockCnpAnticipationOrder.description: Informs whether the merchant has anticipation blocking for CNP transactions.

url.description: URL of the website or social media profile from which the merchant receives CNP purchase orders.

merchant-configuration-request.description: Merchant configuration request model. Object will be required if the merchant has the CNP configuration.

url.description: URL of the website or social media profile from which the merchant receives CNP purchase orders.

merchant-configuration-request.title: merchant-configuration-request

slug.description: Unique merchant identifier.

documentId.description: CPF or CNPJ of the merchant.

slug.description: Unique identification of the agreement term.

userAcceptance.description: Identification of the user who signed the term.

deviceAcceptance.description: Identifier of the channel through which the term was signed.

active.description: Informs whether this term record is active.

termAcceptanceStatus.description: Description of the term signature status.

slug.description: Unique identifier of the price table.

name.description: The name of the rate table.

anticipationType.description: The type of the antecipation of the price table.

listMerchantPriceGroup.description: Object containing the fees by product and brand.

slug.description: Unique identifier of the fee group.

brand.description: The name of the card brand.

groupId.description: The identifier of the group of price fees of the card brand.

compulsoryAnticipationConfig.description: The number of days that will be antecipated. When the table is COMPULSORY.

eventualAnticipationFee.description: Eventual antecipation rate. When the table is EVENTUAL.

cardPixMdr.description: MDR for PIX transactions made through physical channels.

cardPixCeilingFee.description: Maximum fee that can be charged for a PIX transaction through physical channels.

cardPixMinimumCostFee.description: Minimum fee charged for a PIX transaction through physical channels.

nonCardPixMdr.description: MDR for PIX transactions made through online channels.

nonCardPixCeilingFee.description: Maximum fee that can be charged for a PIX transaction through online channels.

nonCardPixMinimumCostFee.description: Minimum fee charged for a PIX transaction through online channels.

dtUpdate.description: Date of the last price table update.

Authorization.name: Authorization
Authorization.description: The integration token will be provided by Dock to the customer. To access the integration token, request our team.
